title: Motor Triac Short Circuit
shortDescription: The motor triac on the main control board has short-circuited, preventing the motor from being controlled properly.
fullDescription: Error F01 indicates a short circuit in the motor triac on the electronic control board. The triac is a semiconductor that regulates power to the motor. When it fails short, the motor may run uncontrolled or not at all. This fault requires inspection of the main PCB and motor wiring.

causes.cause: Motor triac on the control board has failed
Short circuit in motor winding
Damaged wiring between control board and motor
Control board failure due to power surge

symptoms: Motor runs continuously and cannot be stopped
Motor does not start at all
Burning smell from control board area

fixSteps.title: Power reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the washer from mains power for 10 minutes. Reconnect and attempt a new cycle. If F01 returns, further diagnosis is needed.

fixSteps.title: Inspect motor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the rear panel and check all wiring connections between the motor and the main control board. Look for burnt, loose, or damaged connectors.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor connector from the PCB. Measure resistance across motor windings (typically 2-8 ohms). Check for short to ground between windings and motor housing.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If motor tests good but F01 persists, the triac on the main PCB has failed. Replace the entire control board module. Ensure the replacement matches your model number exactly.

whenToCallPro: Control board replacement requires precise connector matching and may need reprogramming. Professional service is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer F01 motor triac error fix

faq.q: Can I repair the triac myself instead of replacing the board?
faq.a: Technically possible if you have electronics soldering skills, but replacing the full board is more reliable and recommended by Ariston.

faq.q: Will a power surge cause F01?
faq.a: Yes, voltage spikes can damage the triac. Using a surge protector is recommended to prevent this fault.

faq.q: Is F01 the same on Hotpoint and Indesit machines?
faq.a: Yes, Ariston, Hotpoint, and Indesit share the same platform and F01 means the same motor triac fault across all three brands.

title: Motor Tacho Circuit Fault
shortDescription: The motor tachometer signal is not being received by the control board.
fullDescription: Error F02 indicates the control board is not receiving a speed signal from the motor tachometer (tacho generator). The tacho is a small sensor on the motor that reports rotation speed. Without this feedback, the control board cannot regulate drum speed safely and halts the cycle.

causes.cause: Tachometer coil on motor has failed
Tachometer magnet has come loose or detached
Wiring from tachometer to control board is broken
Motor carbon brushes worn down preventing rotation

symptoms: Motor hums but does not turn
Washer stops during wash or spin cycle

fixSteps.title: Reset and retry
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine for 10 minutes. Plug back in and run a test cycle. If F02 persists, proceed to check the motor.

fixSteps.title: Check motor carbon brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the rear panel and locate the motor. Extract the carbon brushes from either side. If shorter than 1 cm, they need replacement. Worn brushes are the most common cause of F02.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the tachometer
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the tacho sensor on the rear of the motor. Check that the magnet is secure and the coil is intact. Measure resistance across the tacho coil (typically 100-200 ohms).

fixSteps.title: Check wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring harness from the motor tacho connector to the control board. Ensure all plugs are firmly seated and no wires are broken or corroded.

whenToCallPro: If carbon brushes and tachometer test fine, the control board input circuit may be faulty, requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer F02 tachometer motor brush error fix

faq.q: How often do carbon brushes need replacing?
faq.a: Typically every 5-8 years with normal use. Heavy use with frequent high-speed spins accelerates wear.

faq.q: Can I replace carbon brushes myself?
faq.a: Yes, this is one of the most common DIY washing machine repairs. The brushes slide out from either side of the motor.

faq.q: What does the tachometer do?
faq.a: The tachometer generates a small electrical signal proportional to motor speed, allowing the control board to precisely regulate drum RPM during different cycle phases.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The wash temperature sensor (NTC thermistor) is providing an out-of-range or open circuit reading.
fullDescription: Error F03 indicates the NTC (Negative Temperature Coefficient) thermistor is reading outside its expected range, or the circuit is open. The NTC sensor monitors water temperature so the control board can regulate the heating element. Without a valid temperature reading, the washer cannot heat water safely.

causes.cause: NTC thermistor has failed (open circuit)
Wiring between NTC sensor and control board is broken
NTC connector is corroded or loose on the control board
Control board NTC input circuit has failed

symptoms: Water does not heat during wash cycles
Clothes come out cold even on hot wash
Cycle runs much longer than expected

fixSteps.title: Reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and retry. Temperature sensor faults can sometimes be caused by a temporary glitch.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the NTC sensor in the heating element housing at the bottom of the tub. Disconnect it and measure resistance. At room temperature (20°C), expect approximately 20,000 ohms. Values of infinity or zero indicate a failed sensor.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring and connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the two-wire connection from the NTC to the control board. Check for broken wires, corroded pins, or loose connectors at both ends.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the sensor from the heater housing. Insert the new NTC sensor and reconnect the wiring. Run a hot wash cycle to verify normal operation.

whenToCallPro: Only if both the NTC sensor and wiring test good, suggesting a control board fault.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer F03 NTC temperature sensor replacement

faq.q: What is an NTC sensor?
faq.a: An NTC (Negative Temperature Coefficient) thermistor is a resistor whose resistance decreases as temperature increases. It allows the control board to measure water temperature precisely.

faq.q: Can I wash with F03 showing?
faq.a: The washer may run on some models but will not heat the water properly. Washing in cold water is possible but not ideal for hygiene or stain removal.

faq.q: Is the NTC sensor inside the heating element?
faq.a: On most Ariston washers, the NTC is a separate probe inserted into a pocket in the heater element housing, making it easy to replace independently.

title: Pressure Switch Stuck on Empty
shortDescription: The pressure switch is stuck in the empty position, causing the machine to continuously fill with water.
fullDescription: Error F04 indicates the pressure switch (water level sensor) remains in the 'empty' state even after the fill valves have been open for the expected time. The machine detects it should have filled but the sensor still reads empty. This can lead to overfilling if the mechanical overflow is not triggered.

causes.cause: Pressure switch hose is blocked, kinked, or disconnected
Pressure switch (pressostat) has failed
Pressure switch chamber is clogged with detergent residue
Control board not reading pressure switch signal correctly

symptoms: Cycle does not advance past fill stage
Water visible on floor beneath machine

fixSteps.title: Check the pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the small rubber hose running from the bottom of the tub to the pressure switch (usually on top of the machine). Ensure it is not kinked, cracked, or disconnected. Blow gently through it to check for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Clean the pressure chamber
fixSteps.instruction: Where the hose connects to the tub, there is a small air chamber. Remove the hose and clean any detergent buildup from the chamber using warm water.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the pressure switch. Attach a short piece of tubing to the inlet and blow gently. You should hear a click when sufficient pressure is applied, and another click when released. If no click, the switch is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: If the pressure switch does not click, replace it. Disconnect the wiring connector and hose, remove the mounting clip, and install the new switch.

whenToCallPro: If both the pressure hose and switch test good, the control board may have a faulty input, requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer F04 pressure switch error fix

faq.q: Can F04 cause flooding?
faq.a: Potentially yes. If the pressure switch is stuck on empty, the machine may continue filling. Most machines have a secondary overflow protection, but it is best to address F04 promptly.

faq.q: What is a pressure switch?
faq.a: A pressure switch (pressostat) measures the water level in the tub by sensing air pressure in a tube connected to the tub. As water rises, it compresses air in the tube, triggering the switch.

faq.q: How often should I clean the pressure chamber?
faq.a: Annual cleaning is recommended, especially if you use powder detergent which can cause more buildup in the chamber.

title: Drain Pump Fault
shortDescription: The drain pump is not operating correctly or the pressure switch is stuck in the full position.
fullDescription: Error F05 indicates the pressure switch remains in the 'full' state after the drain pump has operated for the expected duration. The machine believes it still has water despite attempting to drain. This is commonly caused by a blocked drain system or failed pump.

causes.cause: Drain pump filter is clogged with debris
Drain hose is kinked or blocked
Drain pump impeller is jammed or motor has failed
Pressure switch stuck in full position

symptoms: Water remains in the drum after cycle
Drain pump runs but water does not empty
No drain pump sound at all

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the small access panel at the bottom-front of the machine. Place towels and a shallow tray underneath. Slowly unscrew the filter cap and let water drain. Remove any debris, coins, buttons, or lint from the filter.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the machine away from the wall and inspect the drain hose. Ensure it is not kinked, crushed, or pushed too far into the standpipe. Remove the hose from the standpipe and check for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter removed, look inside the pump housing and check if the impeller spins freely. If jammed, clear the obstruction. If free but the pump does not run, test for 230V at the pump connector during a drain cycle.

fixSteps.title: Replace the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump motor has failed, disconnect the electrical connector and the hose clamps. Remove the mounting screws and swap in the new pump. Reconnect everything and run a test drain.

whenToCallPro: If the pump, hose, and filter are all clear but F05 persists, professional diagnosis of the pressure switch and control board is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer F05 drain pump blocked fix

faq.q: How do I drain the water manually?
faq.a: Open the filter access panel at the bottom-front. Place towels and a container below. Slowly unscrew the filter cap to let water drain gradually.

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain filter?
faq.a: Every 1-2 months is recommended. Check more frequently if you wash items that shed a lot of lint or fibers.

faq.q: Can a blocked standpipe cause F05?
faq.a: Yes. If the standpipe (wall drain) is blocked, water backs up into the machine. Clean the standpipe and ensure good airflow around the drain hose connection.

title: Door Lock / Button Fault
shortDescription: The control board has detected a problem with the door lock mechanism or the user interface buttons.
fullDescription: Error F06 covers faults related to the door interlock mechanism or the push-button panel on the user interface. On some models it specifically indicates a button circuit fault; on others it signals the door lock solenoid or microswitch has failed. The machine will not start or will stop mid-cycle.

causes.cause: Door lock (interlock) mechanism has failed
Door lock wiring harness is damaged
Control panel button is stuck or shorted
Door striker or catch is misaligned

symptoms: Door will not lock when cycle is started
Door appears locked but machine does not start
Buttons on control panel do not respond
Machine stops and door will not open

fixSteps.title: Check the door latch
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the door catch and striker plate. Ensure the door closes firmly and the latch engages fully. Check for any debris or damage to the door seal preventing proper closure.

fixSteps.title: Test the door interlock
fixSteps.instruction: Access the door lock from inside the door rim. Disconnect its connector and measure continuity across the interlock terminals with the door closed. Refer to the wiring diagram for the correct pin configuration.

fixSteps.title: Inspect button panel
fixSteps.instruction: If the door lock tests good, check the control panel buttons. Remove the top panel and inspect the button membrane or PCB for stuck buttons, moisture damage, or cracked solder joints.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door interlock
fixSteps.instruction: If the interlock is faulty, peel back the door seal rubber, remove the retaining ring, and extract the interlock. Install the new unit, refit the seal, and test the door lock function.

whenToCallPro: If both the door lock and buttons test fine, the control board may have a faulty relay or input circuit.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer F06 door lock interlock replacement

faq.q: Can I open the door with F06 displayed?
faq.a: If the door is locked, wait a few minutes after turning the machine off. If it still will not open, you may need to use the emergency door release (small tab behind the filter access panel).

faq.q: Is the door interlock the same as the door lock?
faq.a: Yes, the door interlock is the electrical device that locks the door and signals the control board that it is safe to start filling with water.

faq.q: Why would buttons cause F06?
faq.a: On some Ariston models, F06 indicates a fault in the user interface circuit. A stuck or shorted button can send incorrect signals to the main control board.

title: Heater Relay Fault
shortDescription: The heating element relay on the control board is stuck or not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error F07 indicates the control board cannot properly control the heating element relay. The relay may be stuck in the on position (causing continuous heating) or stuck off (no heating). This is a safety-critical fault as uncontrolled heating can damage the machine or clothing.

causes.cause: Heater relay on control board is welded shut or open
Heating element has shorted to ground
NTC sensor providing incorrect reading causing relay confusion
Wiring fault between heater and control board

symptoms: No heating at all during hot wash cycles

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect from mains for 10 minutes. A software glitch can sometimes cause a false F07. If the error returns, continue diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Access the heater at the bottom of the tub (rear panel). Disconnect the heater terminals and measure resistance (typically 20-30 ohms). Also check insulation between each terminal and the heater body (should be over 2 megaohms).

fixSteps.title: Check the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Test the NTC sensor resistance (approximately 20K ohms at room temperature). A faulty NTC can cause the control board to mismanage the heater relay.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the heater and NTC are both good, the relay on the control board has likely failed. Replace the main PCB module.

whenToCallPro: F07 involves safety-critical heating components. If the heater and NTC test normal, professional control board replacement is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer F07 heater relay error fix

faq.q: Is F07 dangerous?
faq.a: It can be if the heater relay is stuck on, causing continuous heating. If you notice steam or overheating, turn off the machine immediately and unplug it.

faq.q: Can I still wash on cold with F07?
faq.a: It is not recommended. If the relay is stuck on, the heater may still activate even on a cold cycle, which is a safety risk.

faq.q: What causes relay failure?
faq.a: Relays are mechanical components that wear over time. A short-circuited heater can also cause the relay contacts to weld together.

title: Heater Relay Stuck On
shortDescription: The heating element relay is stuck in the closed (on) position on the control board.
fullDescription: Error F08 is specifically for the heater relay being stuck in the energized (closed) state. Unlike F07 which covers general relay faults, F08 confirms the relay contacts are welded together, meaning the heater is permanently powered. This is a safety error that immediately stops the machine.

causes.cause: Heater relay contacts are welded shut from electrical arcing
Control board PCB damage near the relay circuit
Heating element drawing excessive current causing relay damage
Power surge damaged the relay

symptoms: Machine stops immediately as safety measure
Heater stays on even when it should be off

fixSteps.title: Immediately unplug the machine
fixSteps.instruction: F08 is a safety fault. Unplug the machine right away. Do not attempt to run any cycles until repaired.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the heater and measure resistance (20-30 ohms normal). Check insulation to ground (must exceed 2 megaohms). A shorted heater causes relay damage.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control board relay
fixSteps.instruction: Visually inspect the relay on the control board for signs of overheating, blackened PCB, or melted plastic. If visible damage is present, the board must be replaced.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board and heater if needed
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the main control board. If the heater tested faulty, replace it as well to prevent the new board from suffering the same relay failure.

whenToCallPro: F08 is a safety-critical fault. Professional repair is strongly recommended to ensure the new relay and heater combination functions safely.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer F08 heater relay stuck error fix

faq.q: Can F08 cause a fire?
faq.a: While modern machines have multiple safety systems, a stuck heater relay is a genuine safety hazard. Unplug the machine immediately and do not use it until repaired.

faq.q: Why does the relay get stuck?
faq.a: Relay contacts can weld together from electrical arcing, especially if the heater draws excessive current due to a developing short circuit. Power surges can also damage relays.

faq.q: Should I replace just the relay or the whole board?
faq.a: Replacing just the relay requires skilled soldering and the PCB may have additional damage. Full board replacement is the standard repair approach.

title: EEPROM / Software Error
shortDescription: The EEPROM memory chip on the control board has a data error or communication fault.
fullDescription: Error F09 indicates a problem with the EEPROM (Electrically Erasable Programmable Read-Only Memory) on the main control board. The EEPROM stores the washer's programming and settings. If this data becomes corrupted, the control board cannot operate the machine correctly.

causes.cause: EEPROM data has become corrupted
Power surge or brownout corrupted the memory
Control board hardware fault affecting EEPROM circuit
Loose connector between display board and main board

symptoms: Machine will not start any cycle
Display shows random or incorrect information

fixSteps.title: Perform a full power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine for 30 minutes (longer than usual to allow capacitors to fully discharge). Reconnect and try starting a cycle. Sometimes EEPROM data recovers after a full power cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check board interconnections
fixSteps.instruction: Open the top panel and check the ribbon cable or connector between the display board and the main control board. Reseat all connectors firmly.

fixSteps.title: Attempt EEPROM reprogramming
fixSteps.instruction: Some Ariston service engineers can reprogram the EEPROM using a diagnostic tool. This requires the correct software file for your specific model and a compatible programmer.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If reprogramming is not possible or fails, replace the entire main control board with a pre-programmed replacement matching your model number.

whenToCallPro: EEPROM faults almost always require professional intervention. Board replacement or reprogramming needs specialized tools and knowledge.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer F09 EEPROM memory error fix

faq.q: What is EEPROM?
faq.a: EEPROM is a type of non-volatile memory chip that stores the washer's firmware, cycle programs, and configuration data. It retains data even when power is off.

faq.q: Can a power cut cause F09?
faq.a: Yes, sudden power loss during a write operation to the EEPROM can corrupt its data. Using a surge protector helps prevent this.

faq.q: Is the replacement board pre-programmed?
faq.a: Genuine Ariston replacement boards come pre-programmed for specific model ranges. Ensure you order the correct part number for your exact model.

title: Pressure Switch No Signal
shortDescription: The pressure switch is not providing any signal to the control board — no empty and no full indication.
fullDescription: Error F10 means the control board receives no valid signal from the pressure switch at all. Unlike F04 (stuck empty) or F05 (stuck full), F10 indicates a complete communication failure between the pressure switch and the control board, often due to a disconnected or failed sensor.

causes.cause: Pressure switch wiring connector is disconnected
Pressure switch has completely failed
Wiring harness between switch and control board is broken
Control board pressure switch input circuit failure

symptoms: Machine does not fill with water
Machine does not start cycle at all
Fill valve opens but cycle does not progress
Error appears immediately on cycle start

fixSteps.title: Check pressure switch connector
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel and locate the pressure switch (round disc-shaped component usually near the top-left). Ensure the electrical connector is firmly attached.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the rubber hose from the tub to the pressure switch is connected at both ends, not cracked or perished. A completely disconnected hose means no signal can reach the switch.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure switch electrically
fixSteps.instruction: With the connector removed, use a multimeter to check continuity across the switch terminals. Blow into the hose port to simulate water pressure — the switch should change state (click).

fixSteps.title: Trace the wiring
fixSteps.instruction: If the switch tests good, trace the wiring from the switch connector back to the control board. Check for any breaks, pinched wires, or damaged insulation.

whenToCallPro: If the switch, hose, and wiring all test good, the control board input circuit has likely failed.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer F10 pressure switch no signal error

faq.q: What is the difference between F04, F05, and F10?
faq.a: F04 means stuck empty, F05 means stuck full, and F10 means no signal at all. F10 is often the simplest to fix as it may be a disconnected wire or hose.

faq.q: Can vibration cause F10?
faq.a: Yes, excessive vibration can shake loose the pressure switch connector or hose. Ensure the machine is level and not overloaded.

faq.q: Where is the pressure switch located?
faq.a: On most Ariston washers, it is a small round disc-shaped component located near the top of the machine, usually on the left side under the top panel.

title: Drain Pump Not Responding
shortDescription: The drain pump is not receiving power or not responding to the control board signal.
fullDescription: Error F11 indicates the control board cannot communicate with or power the drain pump. Unlike F05 which means the pump runs but water does not drain, F11 means the pump circuit itself has a fault — the pump may not run at all. This is an electrical fault in the pump circuit.

causes.cause: Drain pump wiring connector is loose or disconnected
Drain pump motor winding has failed (open circuit)
Control board pump relay or triac has failed
Wiring harness between board and pump is damaged

symptoms: No drain pump sound during drain phase

fixSteps.title: Check pump connections
fixSteps.instruction: Access the drain pump from underneath or behind the machine. Verify the electrical connector is firmly plugged into the pump. Wiggle it to check for loose pins.

fixSteps.title: Test the pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the pump connector and measure resistance across the pump motor terminals. Typical reading is 5-30 ohms. An open circuit (infinite resistance) means the motor winding has failed.

fixSteps.title: Check for voltage at pump
fixSteps.instruction: Reconnect the pump, start a drain cycle, and carefully measure for mains voltage (230V) at the pump connector. If no voltage is present, the control board is not powering the pump.

fixSteps.title: Replace the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump tests open circuit, replace it. Remove the mounting screws, disconnect hoses and wiring, and install the new pump.

whenToCallPro: If the pump tests good but no voltage reaches it during a drain cycle, the control board output circuit has failed and needs professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer F11 drain pump no power error

faq.q: What is the difference between F05 and F11?
faq.a: F05 means the pump runs but water is not draining (physical blockage). F11 means the pump is not running at all (electrical fault in the pump or its circuit).

faq.q: Can I manually drain with F11?
faq.a: Yes, use the drain filter access at the bottom-front of the machine. Place towels and a container underneath and slowly unscrew the filter cap.

faq.q: How long do drain pumps last?
faq.a: Typically 8-12 years. Pumps that frequently run with debris in the filter may fail earlier.

title: Display / Control Board Communication Error
shortDescription: Communication between the display module and the main control board has failed.
fullDescription: Error F12 indicates a loss of communication between the user interface display board and the main electronic control board. These two boards are typically connected by a ribbon cable or wiring harness. When communication fails, the display cannot send user commands to the main board.

causes.cause: Ribbon cable between display and main board is loose
Display board has failed
Main control board communication circuit has failed
Moisture or corrosion on board connectors

symptoms: Display is blank or partially lit
Buttons do not respond to presses
Machine will not accept any commands

fixSteps.title: Reseat the board connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel and locate the ribbon cable or wiring connector between the display board (behind the control panel) and the main board. Disconnect and reconnect it firmly.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for moisture damage
fixSteps.instruction: Check both boards and connectors for any signs of moisture, corrosion, or detergent residue. Clean gently with electronics contact cleaner if needed.

fixSteps.title: Test with a replacement display board
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating does not help, the display board may be faulty. Swap it with a known good unit if available, or proceed to replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: Replace either the display board or main control board depending on which is faulty. If unsure, start with the less expensive display board.

whenToCallPro: If reseating connectors does not resolve the issue and you are unsure which board is faulty, a professional can diagnose with specialized tools.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer F12 display communication error fix

faq.q: Can steam cause F12?
faq.a: Yes, excessive steam from overloading or using too much detergent can cause moisture to reach the display board, leading to communication faults.

faq.q: Is the display board expensive?
faq.a: Display boards typically cost between 40-90, significantly less than the main control board. It is the recommended first replacement.

faq.q: Can I test which board is faulty?
faq.a: Without diagnostic tools, it is difficult. The most cost-effective approach is to start by replacing the interconnect cable, then the display board, then the main board.

title: Dryer Temperature Sensor Fault (Washer-Dryer)
shortDescription: The drying cycle temperature sensor is reporting an out-of-range reading on washer-dryer combo models.
fullDescription: Error F13 applies to Ariston washer-dryer combination units. It indicates the NTC temperature sensor for the drying circuit is open, shorted, or reading outside its expected range. The drying cycle will not function without a valid temperature reading.

causes.cause: Dryer NTC sensor has failed
Dryer NTC wiring is damaged or disconnected
Dryer heating element causing sensor overload
Control board dryer circuit failure

symptoms: F13 error code displayed during drying
Drying cycle does not start or stops early
Clothes remain damp after dry cycle

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine for 10 minutes and retry a drying cycle. If F13 returns, the sensor needs testing.

fixSteps.title: Locate and test the dryer NTC
fixSteps.instruction: The dryer NTC is separate from the wash NTC. It is typically located near the dryer heating element or in the air duct. Disconnect it and measure resistance (approximately 10K-20K ohms at room temperature).

fixSteps.title: Check dryer NTC wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring from the dryer NTC sensor to the control board. Look for heat damage, broken wires, or loose connectors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the dryer NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor is faulty, replace it. Remove the mounting clip, disconnect the wiring, and install the new sensor in the same position.

whenToCallPro: If the NTC and wiring test fine, the control board dryer circuit may be faulty, requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer dryer F13 dryer temperature sensor fix

faq.q: Does F13 only affect the dryer function?
faq.a: Yes, F13 is specific to the drying circuit. The washing function should continue to work normally.

faq.q: Can I still use the washing function?
faq.a: Yes, most models allow you to run wash-only cycles. The dryer function will be disabled until F13 is resolved.

faq.q: Is the dryer NTC different from the wash NTC?
faq.a: Yes, they are separate sensors. The dryer NTC monitors air temperature in the drying circuit, while the wash NTC monitors water temperature.

title: Dryer Heating Element Fault (Washer-Dryer)
shortDescription: The drying cycle heating element is not working on washer-dryer combo models.
fullDescription: Error F14 applies to Ariston washer-dryer units and indicates the dryer heating element is not operating correctly. The element may be open circuit, or the control board cannot activate it. Without the heater, the drying cycle cannot generate the hot air needed to dry clothes.

causes.cause: Dryer heating element has burned out (open circuit)
Thermal cut-out fuse on heater has tripped
Wiring to dryer heater is broken
Control board dryer relay has failed

symptoms: F14 error code displayed during drying
Dryer runs but air is cold
Clothes still wet after drying cycle

fixSteps.title: Reset and check thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes. The dryer heater has a thermal cut-out fuse that may have tripped from overheating. After cooling, it may reset automatically on some models.

fixSteps.title: Test the dryer heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Access the dryer heater (typically behind the rear panel or inside the air duct). Disconnect it and measure resistance. Expect 20-40 ohms. An open circuit (infinity) means the element has failed.

fixSteps.title: Check the thermal fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Test the thermal cut-out fuse on the heater assembly for continuity. If open, the fuse has tripped and needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace heater or fuse
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the failed component. The heater element slides out after removing mounting brackets. The thermal fuse clips onto the heater housing.

whenToCallPro: If both the heater and thermal fuse test good, the control board dryer relay may have failed.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer dryer F14 dryer heater element fix

faq.q: Why does the thermal fuse trip?
faq.a: Overheating due to a blocked lint filter, restricted airflow, or overloading the dryer. Always clean the lint filter before each drying cycle.

faq.q: Can I dry clothes without fixing F14?
faq.a: No, the dryer will only blow cold air. You can still use the washing function.

faq.q: Is the dryer heater different from the wash heater?
faq.a: Yes, they are completely separate. The wash heater is immersed in water at the bottom of the tub, while the dryer heater is in the air duct.

title: Dryer Heater Relay Fault (Washer-Dryer)
shortDescription: The dryer heating element relay on the control board is stuck or malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error F15 on Ariston washer-dryer units indicates the relay controlling the dryer heating element is not functioning correctly. Similar to F07/F08 for the wash heater, this relay may be stuck closed (continuous heating) or stuck open (no heating). This is a control board level fault.

causes.cause: Dryer heater relay contacts welded or stuck
Control board PCB damage in dryer relay circuit
Dryer heater short circuit causing relay damage
Power surge damaged the relay

symptoms: Dryer overheats or produces no heat

fixSteps.title: Safety power off
fixSteps.instruction: If overheating is detected, unplug the machine immediately. Allow it to cool completely before any diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Test the dryer heater
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the dryer heater and check resistance (20-40 ohms) and insulation to ground (over 2 megaohms). A shorted heater can damage the relay.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the relay on the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Visually inspect the dryer relay on the PCB for discoloration, melted plastic, or burnt tracks. These are signs of a failed relay.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the main control board. If the dryer heater also tested faulty, replace both to prevent the same failure recurring.

whenToCallPro: F15 involves safety-critical components. Professional repair is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer dryer F15 dryer relay error fix

faq.q: Is F15 a fire risk?
faq.a: If the relay is stuck on, the dryer heater runs uncontrolled which is a safety hazard. Unplug the machine and do not use the dryer function until repaired.

faq.q: Can I still use the washing function?
faq.a: While the wash function may technically work, using the machine with a known relay fault is not recommended for safety reasons.

faq.q: Can the relay be replaced individually?
faq.a: Technically yes with skilled soldering, but full board replacement is the standard and more reliable approach.

title: Drum Lock Fault (Top-Loader)
shortDescription: The drum lock or brake mechanism on top-loading models is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error F16 on Ariston top-loading washing machines indicates the drum locking mechanism that prevents the drum from rotating during loading has failed. On front-loaders this code may relate to the parking brake sensor. The machine cannot safely spin if the drum is not properly locked.

causes.cause: Drum lock solenoid has failed
Drum lock position sensor fault
Wiring to drum lock mechanism is damaged
Control board drum lock output has failed

symptoms: Drum does not lock in position
Machine will not enter spin cycle
Clicking sound from drum lock area
Top loader lid cannot be opened or closed properly

fixSteps.title: Reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and retry. Sometimes the drum lock sensor misreads after a power glitch.

fixSteps.title: Check drum lock mechanism
fixSteps.instruction: Access the drum lock assembly and check if the solenoid engages when activated. Listen for clicking sounds that indicate it is trying to engage.

fixSteps.title: Test the solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the drum lock solenoid and measure resistance (typically 30-100 ohms). An open circuit means the solenoid coil has burned out.

fixSteps.title: Replace the drum lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the drum lock mounting screws, disconnect the wiring, and install the new assembly. Ensure the lock engages and releases correctly before reassembling.

whenToCallPro: If the drum lock and solenoid test good, the control board may have a faulty output circuit.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston top loader F16 drum lock error fix

faq.q: Is F16 only on top-loading machines?
faq.a: F16 primarily applies to top-loading Ariston models with a drum parking brake. Some front-loader models may use this code differently.

faq.q: Can the drum be manually unlocked?
faq.a: On most models, the drum lock releases when power is removed. Unplug the machine and wait a few minutes.

faq.q: Why does the drum need to be locked?
faq.a: On top-loaders, the drum must be in a specific position for loading and unloading. The lock ensures the drum opening aligns with the lid opening.

title: Door Lock Timeout
shortDescription: The door lock did not engage within the expected time after starting a cycle.
fullDescription: Error F17 indicates the door interlock did not confirm it was locked within the timeout period after the control board sent the lock command. This differs from F06 in that F17 is specifically about the timing — the lock was commanded but did not respond in time.

causes.cause: Door interlock mechanism is slow or intermittent
Door not fully closed when cycle started
Door interlock wiring has high resistance
Mains voltage is too low for interlock solenoid

symptoms: F17 error code displayed shortly after pressing start
Door lock clicks but does not fully engage
Intermittent door locking — sometimes works, sometimes does not

fixSteps.title: Ensure door is fully closed
fixSteps.instruction: Push the door firmly until you hear a solid click. Check that nothing (clothing, door seal) is preventing full closure. Try starting the cycle again.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door catch
fixSteps.instruction: Check the door striker plate and catch for wear or damage. The catch must engage the interlock firmly. Replace if the catch is worn or broken.

fixSteps.title: Test the door interlock
fixSteps.instruction: Access the interlock and check that it is receiving mains voltage when the cycle starts. Also test the feedback contacts for continuity when the lock is engaged.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door interlock
fixSteps.instruction: If the interlock is slow to engage or does not provide feedback, replace it. Peel back the door seal, remove the retaining band, and swap the interlock.

whenToCallPro: If the interlock and door catch are fine, the control board may have a timing issue requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer F17 door lock timeout error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between F06 and F17?
faq.a: F06 is a general door lock or button fault. F17 specifically means the door lock did not engage within the expected time, suggesting a slow or failing interlock mechanism.

faq.q: Can low voltage cause F17?
faq.a: Yes, if the mains voltage is significantly below normal, the interlock solenoid may not have enough power to engage quickly enough, triggering the timeout.

faq.q: How long does the machine wait for the door to lock?
faq.a: Typically 15-30 seconds. If the interlock does not confirm it is locked within this time, F17 is triggered.

title: Main Processor Communication Error
shortDescription: The main processor on the control board has encountered a critical communication or processing error.
fullDescription: Error F18 indicates a serious fault within the main microprocessor on the control board. The processor cannot communicate with the other components on the board or has entered a fault state. This is a non-repairable component-level failure that requires board replacement.

causes.cause: Main processor chip has failed
Power surge has damaged the processor circuit
Solder joint failure on the processor pins
Voltage regulator on the board has failed

fixSteps.title: Full power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine for 30 minutes to allow a full capacitor discharge. Reconnect and check if the machine powers on normally. If F18 returns, the board needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the mains outlet is providing correct voltage (230V). A low or fluctuating supply can cause processor faults. Test with a different outlet if possible.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel and visually inspect the main control board for any burnt components, swollen capacitors, or discolored areas on the PCB.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Order the correct pre-programmed main control board for your model. Disconnect all connectors, remove the old board, install the new one, and reconnect everything.

whenToCallPro: F18 is a board-level fault. If a power reset does not clear it, professional replacement is the only option.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer F18 processor error control board replacement

faq.q: Is the machine worth repairing with F18?
faq.a: A control board costs 80-200 for the part. If the machine is otherwise in good condition and less than 8-10 years old, it is generally worth repairing.

faq.q: Can F18 be caused by a power outage?
faq.a: Yes, sudden power loss or surges can damage the processor. A surge protector is recommended.

faq.q: Do I need the exact same board number?
faq.a: Yes, the board must match your specific model as it comes pre-programmed with the correct firmware for your machine's features and motor type.

title: Water Supply Problem
shortDescription: The machine is not receiving sufficient water supply to fill within the expected time.
fullDescription: Error H20 (displayed as H2O on some models) indicates the washing machine has not filled with enough water within the allowed time. This is typically a water supply issue rather than a machine fault. The fill valves are opening but insufficient water is reaching the tub.

causes.cause: Water supply taps are turned off or not fully open
Inlet hose is kinked or blocked
Inlet hose filter screens are clogged with sediment
Water inlet valve has failed
Low water pressure in the home

symptoms: No water enters the drum at all
Fill valve buzzes but no water flows

fixSteps.title: Check the water supply taps
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure both hot and cold water taps (if applicable) are fully open. Turn them off and on again to confirm water flow.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the inlet hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the machine away from the wall and check the inlet hoses for kinks or crushing. Disconnect the hoses from the back of the machine and check for blockages. Inspect the small mesh filter screens inside the hose connectors and clean them.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet valve filters
fixSteps.instruction: At the back of the machine where the hoses connect, there are usually small mesh filters inside the valve inlets. Carefully remove them with needle-nose pliers and clean under running water.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: If water supply and filters are fine, test the inlet valve. With the machine running a fill cycle, check for 230V at the valve connector. If voltage is present but no water flows, the valve is faulty and needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: Only if the inlet valve needs replacement and you are not comfortable working with water connections near electrical components.
videoSearchQuery: Ariston washer H20 water supply error fix

faq.q: Is H20 the same as H2O?
faq.a: Yes, the display is meant to represent H2O (water). Different models display it slightly differently but it is the same error.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause H20?
faq.a: Yes, if your home water pressure is below about 1 bar, the machine may not fill quickly enough. Check if other taps in the home also have low pressure.

faq.q: Do I need to connect both hot and cold hoses?
faq.a: Most modern Ariston washers only use a cold water connection and heat internally. Check your model manual. If your model has two connections, both must be connected or a blanking cap fitted.
